title: Jason Quinn
content:
People.ContactEmail jbquinn@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Jason obtained his J.D. from the University of Toronto in 2025. Before law school, he completed an Honours Bachelor of Arts in Political Science earning first class honours from the University of Manitoba.
While at the University of Manitoba, Jason was involved in extracurricular activities centred around politics and foreign affairs. He worked as a research assistant to develop a map focusing on Canada’s economic sanctions regime. Outside of school Jason served as the communications chair for the local branch of the Canadian International Council. Additionally, Jason worked in the constituency office of the former leader of the Manitoba Liberal Party.
At law school, Jason participated in the Philip C. Jessup International Law Moot where he and his teammates finished in the top 16 teams in the world. He also served as the co-editor in chief of the Rights Review, a student run journal with the International Human Rights Program at U of T. In years one and two, Jason was actively involved in the Venezuela Accountability Project as both a team member and director, investigating alleged crimes against humanity in Venezuela. For fun, Jason co-authored Jury in a Hurry, an anonymous advice column in the law school newspaper.
When he isn’t working, Jason enjoys playing basketball, cooking, socializing with friends, and visiting his friends, family and dogs in Winnipeg.
